I’m leaving Instagram. With ads nearly every other post, along with influencers hawking products and a ton of suggested posts for brands, this platform is now just one big advertisement. The new treatment of ads in stories, with the ad symbol tiny and all the way in the right hand lower corner was the last straw. This used to be a platform for connection. Now it’s just an advertising platform.

There is a massive database synchronization flaw between the user-facing privacy settings and Meta's backend production servers. Even when privacy controls are explicitly set to "Everyone," the backend logic permanently freezes the account into a restricted state, forcing broken algorithmic folders like "You May Know" and "Hidden Requests" to stay active.

This causes a destructive user interface loop: when trying to accept or clear a pending message request, the UI violently flickers, the action buttons vanish/reappear, and the server rejects the command because it cannot reconcile the hidden folder attributes with the active profile settings.

I have performed a total hardware and software purge. I completely wiped the iOS Keychain credentials, deleted iCloud backup streams, uninstalled/reinstalled the app, verified the cross-platform folder mismatch on separate desktop web browsers, and sat through a 72-hour server lockout.

Reporting this via "Shake to Report" and the standard support logs over 15 times yields absolutely zero action. Unusable.
